titleEn: VIV Select India debuts with global livestock focus
titleZh: VIV Select India 2026首次亮相，聚焦全球畜牧业与动物蛋白产业链
summaryZh: VIV Select India 2026将于2026年4月22-24日在印度新德里Yashobhoomi会展中心首次举办，这是VIV全球平台进入印度市场的首秀。展会为期三天，汇集130多家国内外展商，涵盖自动化、精准系统、动物健康、生物安全、加工及数字工具等全产业链技术。活动得到印度家禽联合会等机构支持，旨在促进技术转移、商业增长和国际合作，尤其关注家禽、乳业及相关畜牧领域。
featuredReason: 该展会聚焦动物蛋白全产业链技术，尤其涵盖自动化、精准系统和数字工具，与智慧畜牧领域高度相关，是了解印度及全球畜牧业技术趋势的重要窗口。
contentFull: Industry and legislation Conferences & Events VIV Select India debuts with global livestock focus New Delhi show links tech, trade across feed-to-food chain 17 April 2026 2 minute read By: Global Ag Media Asia VIV Select India 2026 takes place from 22–24 April 2026 at Yashobhoomi Convention and Expo Centre, New Delhi, introducing the globally established VIV Worldwide platform to the Indian market for the first time. The three day business to business exhibition brings together international and domestic suppliers, industry leaders, and decision makers at a pivotal moment for India’s rapidly expanding animal protein sector.Organised by VNU Exhibitions Europe, the international division of Royal Dutch Jaarbeurs, in strategic partnership with the Poultry Federation of India (PFI), VIV Select India has been developed as a long term platform to support technology transfer, business growth, and international collaboration within India’s livestock and animal protein industries.VIV Select India 2026 features over 130 exhibitors, representing a strong mix of Indian and international companies. Participation spans Europe, the Middle East, and Asia, underscoring India’s growing importance as a destination for innovation, investment, and long term collaboration in animal protein production.The event is supported by a broad coalition of national and regional industry associations, reinforcing its role as a unifying platform for poultry, dairy, and allied livestock sectors.The exhibition floor presents a comprehensive cross section of technologies and services designed to enhance productivity, efficiency, sustainability, and product quality across animal protein production. Visitors can expect solutions ranging from automation and precision systems to animal health, biosecurity, processing, and digital tools.International and Indian companies such as Big Dutchman, JBT Marel India, Viscon Hatchery Automation, De Heus Animal Nutrition India, FAMSUN, Venky’s India, and Himalaya Wellness Company are among those confirmed—alongside many other technology providers serving integrators, producers, processors, and service companies.VIV Square opens with a formal inaugural ceremony marked by the presence of senior industry leaders and government representatives, including Mr. Jeroen van Hooff, President & CEO of Royal Dutch Jaarbeurs and VNU Group, Mr. Ranpal Dhanda, President of the Poultry Federation of India, and Prof. S.P. Singh Baghel, Honorable Minister of State for Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying. The opening is further distinguished by participation from key public and diplomatic stakeholders such as Ms. Varsha Joshi, Additional Secretary, Department of Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, H.E. Ms. Marisa Gerards, Ambassador of the Kingdom of the Netherlands, and Shri Mahipal Dhanda, Education Minister of Haryana.Across the three days, the programme includes expert-led sessions addressing critical developments in poultry production, dairy advancement, and animal health. Industry leaders from companies including Viscon Hatchery Automation, De Heus Animal Nutrition, JBT Marel, Venkateshwara Hatcheries (Ventri Biologicals), MSD Animal Health, HIPRA, CEVA, Holm & Laue, Binsar Farms, and Verka Dairy are all to share insights on topics such as automation and AI in production systems, nutrition strategies, processing performance, international dairy collaboration, and advances in vaccines and biologicals.Patrick van Rooij, Project Manager - VIV Select India shares, “The poultry and livestock sectors are entering a phase where scale must be matched by efficiency, resilience, and smarter use of technology. VIV Select India has been developed to support that shift—by connecting the value chain, facilitating knowledge exchange, and giving professionals direct access to solutions that work in real production environments. This platform is as much about dialogue and learning as it is about business. Our goal is to create conversations that lead to stronger partnerships, better decisions, and long term value for the industry as a whole.” Our Partners PIC dsm-firmenich Lanxess Hamlet Protein Global Ag Media More from this author More News View all Hog futures climb as pork cutout tops $101 - CME Market Trends Markets and economics 16 July 2026 1 minute read US to impose 25% tariff on most Brazilian imports from July 22 Markets Imports and Exports Trade 16 July 2026 2 minute read Beef, coffee and aircraft exempted as Lula vows WTO challenge Hog futures edge higher as pork cutout slips - CME Market Trends Markets and economics 15 July 2026 1 minute read Cattle futures hit lowest close since March Our Partners PIC dsm-firmenich Lanxess Hamlet Protein
translationZh:
VIV Select India 2026将于2026年4月22日至24日在新德里Yashobhoomi会展中心举行，这是VIV全球平台首次进入印度市场。这场为期三天的B2B展览汇聚了国际和国内供应商、行业领袖及决策者，正值印度快速扩张的动物蛋白产业的关键时刻。

该展会由荷兰皇家Jaarbeurs国际分部VNU Exhibitions Europe组织，与印度家禽联合会（PFI）战略合作，旨在打造一个长期平台，支持印度畜牧业和动物蛋白产业的技术转移、商业增长和国际合作。

VIV Select India 2026将展出超过130家参展商，涵盖印度和国际公司，参展范围遍及欧洲、中东和亚洲，凸显印度作为动物蛋白生产领域创新、投资和长期合作目的地的重要性日益增长。

该活动得到众多国家和区域行业协会的支持，强化了其作为家禽、乳业及相关畜牧行业统一平台的作用。

展会现场将展示一系列旨在提升动物蛋白生产效率、可持续性和产品质量的技术与服务，涵盖自动化、精准系统、动物健康、生物安全、加工及数字工具等解决方案。

已确认参展的国际和印度公司包括Big Dutchman、JBT Marel India、Viscon Hatchery Automation、De Heus Animal Nutrition India、FAMSUN、Venky's India和Himalaya Wellness Company等，此外还有众多为整合商、生产商、加工商和服务企业提供技术的供应商。

**VIV Square开幕仪式**

VIV Square将举行正式开幕仪式，出席嘉宾包括荷兰皇家Jaarbeurs及VNU集团总裁兼CEO Jeroen van Hooff先生、印度家禽联合会主席Ranpal Dhanda先生、印度渔业、畜牧业和乳业部国务部长S.P. Singh Baghel教授。开幕式还邀请了重要公共和外交利益相关方，如印度畜牧业和乳业部附加秘书Varsha Joshi女士、荷兰王国大使Marisa Gerards女士阁下、哈里亚纳邦教育部长Shri Mahipal Dhanda。

在为期三天的活动中，议程包括专家主导的会议，讨论家禽生产、乳业等关键发展议题。
contentHtml:
VIV Select India debuts with global livestock focus 
New Delhi show links tech, trade across feed-to-food chain
